ogTitle: McKellars Park vs McLeans Ridges: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in McKellars Park and McLeans Ridges.
heading: McKellars Park vs McLeans Ridges — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between McKellars Park, New South Wales and McLeans Ridges, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: McKellars Park has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to McLeans Ridges (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is McKellars Park or McLeans Ridges safer from flooding?
faq.answer: McKellars Park has a flood risk score of 37/100, while McLeans Ridges scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: McKellars Park scores 44/100 for bushfire risk. McLeans Ridges scores 65/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in McKellars Park or McLeans Ridges?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. McKellars Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
